title: The Hackathon Story
description: The project story from start to finish — how a single-lane test track became a thousand-lane highway for financial contract evaluation, told through the car factory analogy.
blurb: Follow the journey from a single-threaded ACTUS prototype to a massively parallel GPU engine — covering the motivation, architecture decisions, trade-offs, and benchmark results that defined the project

title: ACTUS Organization
description: The ACTUS Financial Research Foundation — the open standard for financial contract representation, its taxonomy, data dictionary, technical specification, and the organizations behind it.
blurb: Understand the ACTUS open standard — its contract taxonomy of ~32 types, data dictionary, state transition functions, business rules, and the foundation that maintains the specification

title: Background — GPU Computing
description: What a GPU is, how it differs from a CPU, and why GPU architecture is well-suited for large-scale financial contract evaluation using ILGPU.
blurb: Build intuition for why GPUs are a natural fit for bulk financial contract evaluation — comparing CPU and GPU architectures, and introducing ILGPU as the bridge from C# to CUDA and OpenCL

title: Technical Documentation
description: Full implementation reference — core engine architecture, PAM contract processor, state machine, financial conventions, schedule generation, GPU acceleration, demos, and testing.
blurb: Deep-dive into the implementation — the PAM contract engine, state machine, schedule generation, risk factor model, GPU data structures and kernels, sample demos, and the full test suite
features: Core Engine — PAM Contract, State Machine, Conventions
GPU Acceleration — Data Structures, Kernels, Executors

title: ACTUS Insurance Extensions
description: How the ACTUS framework was extended to model insurance contracts — life insurance projection, Markov state transitions, and a domain-specific language for product rules.
blurb: Explore how ACTUS was extended into insurance — projecting life insurance cashflows on GPU using Markov state transitions and a domain-specific language that encodes product rules without code changes
